/technical/sma.description: Calculate the Simple Moving Average over the trailing window.
/technical/sma.examples:
Examples
--------

```python
from openbb import obb
# 50-bar SMA on TSLA close.
data = obb.equity.price.historical(symbol='TSLA', start_date='2023-01-01', provider='fmp').results
sma = obb.technical.sma(data=data, length=50)
obb.technical.sma(data='[{'date': '2023-01-02', 'open': 110.0, 'high': 120.0, 'low': 100.0, 'close': 115.0, 'volume': 10000.0}, {'date': '2023-01-03', 'open': 165.0, 'high': 180.0, 'low': 150.0, 'close': 172.5, 'volume': 15000.0}, {'date': '2023-01-04', 'open': 146.67, 'high': 160.0, 'low': 133.33, 'close': 153.33, 'volume': 13333.33}, {'date': '2023-01-05', 'open': 137.5, 'high': 150.0, 'low': 125.0, 'close': 143.75, 'volume': 12500.0}, {'date': '2023-01-06', 'open': 132.0, 'high': 144.0, 'low': 120.0, 'close': 138.0, 'volume': 12000.0}]', length=2)
```

/technical/rsi.description: Calculate the Relative Strength Index (RSI), Wilder's momentum oscillator.
/technical/rsi.examples:
Examples
--------

```python
from openbb import obb
# RSI(14) on daily TSLA closes.
```

standard.name: data
standard.type: Data | DataFrame | Series | dict | list | list['DataFrame'] | list['Series'] | list[Data] | ndarray
standard.description: The data to be used for the relative rotation calculations. This should be the multi-symbol output from the 'equity.price.historical' endpoint, or similar, at a daily interval. Or a pivot table with the 'date' column as the index, the symbols as the columns, and the 'study' as the values. It is recommended to use the 'equity.price.historical' endpoint to get the data, and feed the results as-is.

standard.name: benchmark
standard.description: The symbol to be used as the benchmark.

standard.name: study
standard.description: The data point for the calculations. If 'price', the closing price will be used. If 'volatility', the standard deviation of the closing price will be used. If 'data' is supplied as a pivot table, the 'study' will assume the values are the closing price and 'volume' will be ignored.

standard.name: long_period
standard.description: The length of the long period for momentum calculation, by default is 252. Adjust this value, to 365, when supplying assets such as crypto.

standard.name: short_period
standard.description: The length of the short period for momentum calculation, by default is 21. Adjust this value, to 30, when supplying assets such as crypto.

standard.name: window
standard.description: The length of window for the standard deviation calculation, by default is 21. Adjust this value, to 30, when supplying assets such as crypto.

standard.name: trading_periods
standard.description: The number of trading periods per year, for the standard deviation calculation, by default is 252. Adjust this value, to 365, when supplying assets such as crypto.

standard.name: chart_params
standard.description:
Additional parameters to pass when `chart=True` and the `openbb-charting` extension is installed. Parameters can be passed again to redraw the chart using the charting.to_chart() method of the response.

            ChartParams
            -----------
            date: Optional[str]
                A target end date within the data, by default is the last date in the data.
            show_tails: bool
                Show the tails on the chart, by default is True.
            tail_periods: Optional[int]
                Number of periods to show in the tails, by default is 16.
            tail_interval: Literal['day', 'week', 'month']
                Interval to show the tails, by default is 'week'.
            title: Optional[str]
                Title of the chart.
